1.en: Few scattered brown spots on lower leaves, less than 5% leaf area affected

2.en: Brown circular lesions with yellow halos on 5-15% of lower and middle canopy leaves

3.en: Lesions spreading to upper canopy, 15-30% defoliation beginning, spots coalescing

4.en: Severe defoliation 30-60%, lesions on petioles and stems, significant canopy thinning

5.en: Over 60% defoliation, premature plant senescence, near-total canopy loss

organic.dosage: 4 g/L water foliar or 2.5 kg/ha soil drench

cultural.en: Rotate crops with non-legumes (cereals) for at least 2 seasons
Remove and destroy infected plant debris after harvest to reduce inoculum

1.en: Small dark brown to black spots on lower leaf surfaces, less than 5% area affected

2.en: Spots enlarge with dark sporulation on abaxial surface, 5-15% leaf area affected

3.en: Lesions on both leaf surfaces, 15-30% defoliation, spots visible on upper canopy

4.en: Severe defoliation 30-60%, lesions on petioles, extensive sporulation

5.en: Over 60% defoliation, near-total canopy collapse, peg and pod quality severely impacted

cultural.en: Use resistant/tolerant varieties such as GPBD-4 or ICGV 86699 where available
Ensure proper plant spacing to improve airflow and reduce leaf wetness duration

1.en: Few orange-brown pustules on lower leaf surfaces, less than 5% leaf area affected

2.en: Pustules increasing, orange-brown uredinia on 5-15% of leaves, mostly lower canopy

3.en: Pustules spreading to upper canopy, 15-30% leaf area covered, early defoliation starting

4.en: Heavy pustule coverage 30-60%, significant defoliation, leaves turning necrotic

5.en: Over 60% leaf area affected, massive defoliation, plants appear scorched

organic.dosage: 25-30 kg/ha dusting or 3 g/L wettable sulfur

cultural.en: Plant early-maturing and rust-resistant varieties like ICGV 86590
Avoid late sowing which exposes crop to peak rust season

1.en: Isolated white mycelial fans at soil line on 1-2 plants per 10m row

2.en: White mycelium and small tan sclerotia visible on stems of 5-10% plants

3.en: Wilting in patches, 10-25% plants showing stem girdling and sclerotial mats

4.en: Major wilting, 25-50% plants collapsed, abundant sclerotia on soil surface

5.en: Over 50% stand loss, entire sections dead, soil surface covered with sclerotia

organic.dosage: 5 kg/ha mixed with FYM before sowing + 2 g/L soil drench at 30 DAS
organic.frequency: Preventive at planting, repeat at 30 and 60 DAS

chemical.dosage: 1.5 mL/L water as soil drench around stem base
chemical.frequency: At disease onset, repeat after 21 days if needed

cultural.en: Practice deep plowing to bury sclerotia below 15 cm depth
Avoid excessive irrigation and ensure proper field drainage

1.en: No visible symptoms on plant, detectable only via lab aflatoxin test at trace levels (<4 ppb)

2.en: Slight yellow-green sporulation on isolated pods, aflatoxin 4-20 ppb

3.en: Visible greenish-yellow Aspergillus colonies on 10-20% pods, aflatoxin 20-100 ppb

4.en: Widespread kernel discoloration and mold, 20-50% pods contaminated, aflatoxin 100-500 ppb

5.en: Massive contamination, over 50% pods affected, aflatoxin >500 ppb, crop unmarketable

organic.frequency: Once at 40-60 DAS before pod development

chemical.dosage: 2 g/kg seed as dry seed treatment

cultural.en: Harvest at optimal maturity, avoid late harvest which increases aflatoxin risk
Dry pods immediately after harvest to below 8% moisture and store in ventilated conditions

1.en: Faint net-like tan lesions on a few lower leaves, less than 5% leaf area

2.en: Web-patterned lesions clearly visible on 5-15% of leaves, mostly lower canopy

3.en: Irregular dark brown blotches with web pattern on 15-30% of leaves, upper canopy involved

4.en: Coalescing necrotic web blotches on 30-60% of leaf area, moderate defoliation

5.en: Over 60% of leaves showing extensive web blotch necrosis, heavy defoliation

cultural.en: Remove crop residues and volunteer peanut plants after harvest
Maintain 2-3 year rotation with non-host crops like sorghum or maize

1.en: Isolated seedling collapse, 1-2 plants per 10m row showing dark brown lesion at collar region

2.en: 5-10% seedling mortality, black discoloration at soil line, some pre-emergence losses

3.en: 10-25% plant mortality, collar region girdled, black Aspergillus sporulation visible

4.en: 25-50% stand loss in patches, extensive collar girdling and plant collapse

5.en: Over 50% stand loss, replanting required, massive pre- and post-emergence mortality

cultural.en: Use certified disease-free seed and treat seed with biocontrol agents before planting
Avoid planting in fields with recent history of collar rot, maintain 3-year rotation

1.en: Isolated plants showing slight leaf curling and mild chlorotic mottle, less than 2% incidence

2.en: Stunting visible in scattered plants (5-10%), leaves showing mosaic and downward curling

3.en: 10-25% plants with severe stunting, rosette symptoms (bushy dwarfing), chlorotic or green rosette types

4.en: 25-50% plants severely stunted and non-productive, heavy aphid vector populations present

5.en: Over 50% incidence, widespread plant stunting and sterility, near-total yield loss in affected patches

name.en: Neem seed kernel extract for aphid control
name.hi: नीम बीज गिरी अर्क एफिड नियंत्रण

organic.dosage: 5% NSKE (50 g neem kernel powder per liter water)
organic.frequency: Every 7-10 days during aphid peak (20-40 DAS)

cultural.en: Plant early at recommended date to escape peak aphid vector flights
Use resistant varieties like ICG 12991 or ICGV-SM 90704 where available

1.en: Occasional plants with mild necrotic rings on young leaves, less than 2% incidence

2.en: Necrotic spots on terminal buds in 5-10% of plants, mild axillary shoot proliferation

3.en: 10-25% plants showing terminal bud death, axillary branching, and stunting

4.en: 25-50% plants with dead terminal buds, severe stunting, mottled and distorted leaves

5.en: Over 50% incidence, widespread bud necrosis and plant death, negligible pod set

name.en: Blue/yellow sticky traps for thrips monitoring
name.hi: थ्रिप्स निगरानी के लिए चिपचिपा जाल

organic.frequency: Install at sowing, replace every 2 weeks

chemical.frequency: At thrips onset, repeat after 10-14 days

cultural.en: Plant early to avoid peak thrips vector activity (Thrips palmi)
Intercrop with fast-growing cereals like pearl millet as barrier crop to reduce thrips immigration

1.en: Mild green mottle/mosaic on young leaves of scattered plants, less than 5% incidence

2.en: Clear light and dark green mosaic on 5-15% of plants, slight leaf distortion

3.en: Mottle and mild stunting in 15-30% plants, some leaf puckering and vein banding

4.en: 30-50% plants with obvious mosaic, stunting, and reduced pod fill

5.en: Over 50% plants affected with severe mosaic, stunting, and poor seed quality

name.hi: एफिड रोकथाम के लिए परावर्तक मल्च

organic.frequency: Applied at planting, maintained through 45 DAS

name.en: Imidacloprid foliar for aphid vector control

chemical.frequency: At aphid appearance, max 2 applications 14 days apart

cultural.en: Use virus-free certified seed from known healthy seed sources
Rogue and remove symptomatic plants early (before 30 DAS) to reduce within-field spread

1.en: Occasional plant wilting during midday heat, recovering by evening, less than 2% incidence

2.en: Persistent wilting in 5-10% plants, leaves drooping without yellowing, vascular browning in stem cross-section

3.en: 10-25% plants permanently wilted and dying, brown ooze from cut stems in water test

4.en: 25-50% stand loss, entire rows collapsing, rapid spread along irrigation channels

5.en: Over 50% plants dead, field sections abandoned, soil heavily contaminated

organic.dosage: 2-4 tonnes/ha to raise soil pH above 6.5
organic.frequency: Once before planting, based on soil test

chemical.frequency: At first symptoms, repeat after 10 days, max 3 applications

cultural.en: Practice long rotation (4-5 years) with non-solanaceous, non-leguminous crops like rice or maize
Improve field drainage and avoid waterlogging which facilitates bacterial spread

1.en: Mild stunting in sandy patches, root galls small and few, visible only on uprooted plants

2.en: Patchy yellowing and slight stunting in 5-15% of field, moderate root galling

3.en: Obvious stunted patches covering 15-30% of field, heavy root galling, pods with pegged appearance

4.en: 30-50% of plants severely stunted with extensive galling, pod pegs damaged, secondary rot infections

5.en: Over 50% field showing severe decline, massive root galling, plant death in patches, pod discoloration

organic.dosage: 5 kg/ha mixed with FYM at sowing

cultural.en: Rotate with non-host crops like sorghum, maize, or sesame for 2-3 seasons
Deep summer plowing to expose nematode eggs to desiccation and UV radiation

1.en: Faint interveinal yellowing on youngest leaves, veins remain green

2.en: Clear interveinal chlorosis on new growth, older leaves still green

3.en: Young leaves uniformly pale yellow with distinct green veins, some bleaching

4.en: Severe chlorosis progressing to white/bleached young leaves, growth retarded

5.en: Terminal leaves white and necrotic, plant stunted severely, may die if uncorrected

organic.dosage: 5 g/L water + 2.5 g/L citric acid as adjuvant
organic.frequency: 2-3 sprays at 10-day intervals starting at symptom onset

chemical.dosage: 10-20 g per plant soil application or 2 g/L foliar
chemical.frequency: Once at symptom onset, repeat after 3 weeks if needed

cultural.en: Lower soil pH with sulfur or ammonium-based fertilizers to improve iron availability
Avoid over-liming and ensure adequate organic matter to chelate soil iron naturally

1.en: Slight overall pale green color, older leaves marginally lighter than normal

2.en: Older leaves clearly yellowing (uniform chlorosis), plant growth slightly reduced

3.en: Pronounced yellowing of older leaves progressing upward, reduced branching and canopy

4.en: Severe chlorosis with lower leaf necrosis and drop, stunted plants, poor pod set

5.en: Generalized chlorosis throughout plant, extensive leaf senescence, minimal pod development

organic.dosage: 200 g inoculant per 10 kg seed, slurry coat before sowing

chemical.frequency: 2 sprays at 7-day intervals, avoid during flowering

cultural.en: Inoculate seed with Bradyrhizobium to ensure effective nitrogen fixation
Apply starter nitrogen (20 kg N/ha) only if soil is known to lack native rhizobia

1.en: Slightly reduced pod fill, 5-10% empty or dark-skinned pods at harvest

2.en: 10-20% pods with poor fill, dark inner shell, occasional pops visible

3.en: 20-35% pops/unfilled pods, shriveled kernels, dark pod inner surface common

4.en: 35-50% empty pods, significant kernel abortion, yield seriously reduced

5.en: Over 50% pops, most pods empty or poorly filled, kernels dark and unmarketable

organic.dosage: 400-500 kg/ha broadcast at pegging stage (40-50 DAS)

chemical.frequency: 2 sprays at pegging and pod development stages

cultural.en: Apply gypsum at pegging (40-50 DAS) to ensure calcium availability in the pod zone
Maintain soil pH between 5.8 and 6.5 for optimal calcium uptake

1.en: Internal darkening of cotyledons visible only on cut kernel examination, no external symptoms

2.en: Hollow heart visible in 10-20% of kernels, slight stunting of terminal buds

3.en: 20-35% kernels with hollow heart, thickened brittle leaves, poor flower set

4.en: 35-50% kernels affected, shortened internodes, cracked stems, severely reduced pod set

5.en: Over 50% kernels hollow, terminal bud death, plant severely stunted and deformed

organic.dosage: 10-15 kg/ha borax broadcast and incorporated before sowing
organic.frequency: Once at land preparation, based on soil test

chemical.frequency: 2 sprays: at flowering (35-40 DAS) and at pegging (50-55 DAS)

cultural.en: Conduct soil and tissue boron tests before planting; target soil B above 0.5 ppm
Avoid over-liming which can induce boron deficiency; maintain pH below 7.0

1.en: Mild midday wilting with full recovery by morning, leaves slightly droopy

2.en: Persistent leaf folding/rolling, delayed recovery, slight growth reduction

3.en: Pronounced wilting, lower leaf yellowing and drop, flowering and pegging reduced

4.en: Severe wilting, extensive premature leaf senescence, poor pod fill, plant semi-dormant

5.en: Irreversible wilting, widespread plant death, pods shriveled, near-total yield loss

organic.dosage: 5-7 cm layer of straw/crop residue mulch between rows
organic.frequency: Applied at sowing or at canopy closure
2-3 sprays during dry spells at 10-day intervals

chemical.frequency: Spray at onset of drought stress, repeat after 10 days

IN: Salicylic acid (AR grade from agri suppliers)

cultural.en: Adopt supplemental irrigation at critical stages: flowering (35-50 DAS) and pod fill (70-95 DAS)
Select drought-tolerant varieties like ICGV 91114 or TAG 24 for rain-fed conditions

1.en: Mild leaf cupping or crinkling on new growth, plants recover within 7-10 days

2.en: Noticeable leaf distortion, yellowing or bleaching in patches, slight stunting

3.en: Significant leaf burn/necrosis, stem twisting or epinasty, moderate stunting across 15-30% of field

4.en: Severe foliar necrosis, growth cessation, 30-50% of plants visibly damaged, poor pod set

5.en: Widespread plant death or severe deformation, over 50% stand loss, replanting may be needed

organic.dosage: 200-400 kg/ha surface application and light incorporation

chemical.frequency: 1-2 sprays after herbicide injury symptoms appear

cultural.en: Irrigate thoroughly to leach herbicide residue below the root zone if soil-applied
Apply foliar nutrients (NPK 19-19-19 at 5 g/L) to support plant recovery after injury
